title: Bridge Essay: Sense of Agency Beyond Sensorimotor Process: Decoding Self-Other Action Attribution in the Human Brain.
concept: Sense of agency — voluntary action, free will as constructed experience, intentional binding.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nested Agency** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nested agency — voluntary action, free will as constructed experience, intentional binding.
**Phase context:** Sense of agency — voluntary action, free will as constructed experience, intentional binding.

**Sense of Agency Beyond Sensorimotor Process: Decoding Self-Other Action Attribution in the Human Brain.** — Cereb Cortex (2020). DOI: 10.1093/cercor/bhaa028. PMID: 32188970. The sense of agency is defined as the subjective experience that "I" am the one who is causing the action. Theoretical studies postulate that this subjective experience is developed through multistep processes extending from the sensorimotor to the cognitive level. However, it remains unclear how the brain processes such different levels of information and constitutes the neural substrates for the sense of agency.
